title: 10 best herbal tinctures for overeating

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Guduchi tinctures helps with overeating because it has a natural appetite-suppressing effect, which can help individuals who struggle with emotional eating or bingeing. The herb's ability to calm the nervous system and reduce stress hormones also contributes to its ability to regulate hunger pangs, allowing for healthier food choices. Additionally, Guduchi tinctures have been shown to improve digestion and metabolism, making it easier to feel full and satisfied after meals, reducing the likelihood of overindulgence.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Guduchi's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to emotional eating and cravings for comfort foods.
Adaptogenic properties: As an adaptogen, Guduchi helps the body adapt to stress by regulating cortisol levels, which can lead to overeating as a response to emotional stress.
Appetite-suppressant properties: Guduchi has been shown to reduce appetite and increase feelings of fullness, making it easier to stick to a healthy diet and avoid overindulgence in unhealthy foods.
remedy_constituents: Glycosides: Glycosides in Gymnema sylvestre tinctures, specifically gymnemic acid, help reduce overeating by suppressing the sweetness perception and delaying gastric emptying, leading to reduced caloric intake.

Alkaloids: Alkaloids present in Gymnema sylvestre, such as gymnemic acid and gymnena, help control overeating by reducing carbohydrate absorption, slowing gastric emptying, and improving insulin sensitivity.
Terpenoids: Terpenoids in Gymnema sylvestre tinctures, including gymnemic acid and gymnena, aid in weight management by reducing fat absorption, suppressing appetite, and modulating lipid metabolism.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are considered the most potent part of the plant and are often used to make tinctures due to their high concentration of gymnemic acid, which is believed to help regulate appetite.
Roots: The roots of Gymnema sylvestre are also used to make tinctures, as they contain gymnemic acid and other compounds that are thought to help suppress appetite and reduce sugar cravings.
Stems: The stems of the plant are sometimes used to make tinctures, as they contain smaller amounts of gymnemic acid and other compounds that may help regulate appetite and metabolism.
remedy_recipe: Harvest gymnema sylvestre leaves and stems in the morning after dew has fallen to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the harvested gymnema sylvestre leaves and stems in a warm dark place for 7 to 10 days.
Grind the dried gymnema sylvestre leaves and stems into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Combine 1 part of the fine gymnema sylvestre powder with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the gymnema sylvestre tincture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks before straining and using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tinctures helps with overeating because it aids in digestion, reducing bloating and discomfort after meals. The natural compounds found in fennel seed work to stimulate digestive enzymes, easing the movement of food through the digestive system and promoting a sense of fullness and satisfaction. This can help curb excessive eating habits by reducing cravings for seconds or snacking between meals, making it a useful tool for maintaining a healthy weight and overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Carminative effects: Fennel tincture helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramping and bloating that can contribute to feelings of discomfort after eating too much.

Digestive enzyme stimulation: Fennel's essential oils stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, breaking down food more efficiently and preventing the buildup of undigested nutrients that can lead to overeating.

Satiety-inducing properties: The anethole in fennel tincture has been shown to increase feelings of fullness and satisfaction, reducing the likelihood of overindulging or reaching for unhealthy snacks.
remedy_constituents: Anethole: Acts as an appetite suppressant, helping to reduce cravings and feelings of hunger.
Foeniculin: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which may aid in regulating digestion and reducing overeating caused by inflammation in the digestive tract.
Phenolic acids: May help regulate appetite and satiety by modulating gut hormones, such as ghrelin and leptin, which play a crucial role in hunger and fullness signals.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Used to make tinctures for overeating due to their carminative and antispasmodic properties, which help alleviate digestive discomfort.
Leaves: Utilized in tinctures for overeating due to their ability to ease digestive issues and promote relaxation.
Seeds: The seeds are also used due to their ability to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion and bloating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of foeniculum vulgare roots and leaves at the peak of their potency in late summer.
Cut the harvested foeniculum vulgare into small pieces to increase the surface area for tincture extraction.
Combine 1 pound of the cut foeniculum vulgare with 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discard the solids and store in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle tinctures helps with overeating because they support liver function, which plays a crucial role in regulating appetite and metabolism. The silymarin compounds found in milk thistle have been shown to improve insulin sensitivity and reduce inflammation in the body, both of which can contribute to cravings and overconsumption of food. By promoting healthy liver function, milk thistle tinctures may help individuals develop healthier eating habits and a more balanced relationship with food.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: Acts as an appetite suppressant by reducing the production of ghrelin, a hormone that stimulates hunger, thereby helping to curb excessive eating.
Flavonoids and Polyphenols: Possess antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive system, which may contribute to overeating habits, and promote overall gut health.

Alkaloids: May help regulate blood sugar levels, which can be a trigger for overeating in some individuals, by inhibiting the release of glucose into the bloodstream and improving insulin sensitivity.
remedy_constituents: Silymarin: A complex mixture of flavonoids, silymarin has been shown to improve appetite regulation and reduce food cravings by modulating the gut-brain axis and influencing appetite hormones such as ghrelin and leptin.

Flavonoids (specifically, kaempferol and isorhapontigenin): These polyphenolic compounds have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate the stress and anxiety associated with overeating, thereby reducing the urge to consume excessive food.

Silychristin: A saponin glycoside present in Silybum marianum, silychristin has been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which may contribute to its potential benefits in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with overeating and metabolic disorders.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used due to their high concentration of silymarin, a compound believed to aid in digestion and reduce inflammation.
Leaves: The leaves are used in tinctures because they contain flavonoids and other compounds that may help regulate appetite and improve digestive health.
Stems: The stems of Silybum marianum are used to make tinctures due to their content of bioactive compounds, which may help alleviate symptoms of overeating, such as bloating and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest silybum marianum flowers and leaves from the plant when they are in full bloom and dry them completely.
Combine 1 part dried silybum marianum flowers and leaves with 2 parts 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking it gently every day to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids and reserve the tincture.
Label and store the silybum marianum tincture in a cool dark place away from children and pets indefinitely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with overeating because it supports digestive health by stimulating digestive enzymes and reducing inflammation in the gut. By promoting a healthy balance of gut bacteria, dandelion tincture can also help regulate appetite and reduce cravings for unhealthy foods. Additionally, its diuretic properties can help alleviate water retention and bloating, making it easier to feel full and satisfied after meals. As a result, dandelion tincture can be a helpful natural aid in managing overeating and promoting a healthier relationship with food.
remedy_properties: Bitter principles: Dandelion tincture contains bitter compounds like taraxasterol, which can stimulate the digestive system and reduce cravings for sweet or starchy foods, helping to curb overeating.
Diuretic properties: Dandelion's diuretic effects can help alleviate water retention, reducing feelings of bloating and discomfort that can contribute to overeating and poor food choices.
Cholagogue activity: The tincture's ability to stimulate bile production and release can aid digestion and reduce symptoms like indigestion and discomfort, which may lead to overconsumption of food.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: Help with overeating by reducing inflammation in the digestive system, which may alleviate symptoms of overeating and support digestive health.
Taraxasterols: Aid in regulating appetite and reducing overeating by inhibiting the production of hormones that stimulate appetite, such as ghrelin.

Flavonoids (specifically, kaempferol): Contribute to weight management by reducing inflammation and improving insulin sensitivity, which may help alleviate metabolic issues associated with overeating.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used to make tinctures due to their diuretic and bitter properties, which help reduce water retention and stimulate digestion.
Roots: The roots are used to make tinctures due to their ability to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion and bloating.
Flowers: The flowers are used to make tinctures due to their ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive discomfort associated with overeating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated with 1:5 ratio.
Chop the harvested roots and leaves into small pieces and dry them in a warm place for 2 days.
Combine 1 part of the dried plant material with 2 parts of 80 proof ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and shake it every day for 2 weeks.
Strain the liquid and discard the solids then dilute the tincture with distilled water to desired strength.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild indigo tinctures helps with overeating because it possesses a unique ability to calm the digestive system and reduce cravings. The herb's natural antibacterial properties help to soothe an irritated gut, leading to improved digestion and reduced bloating. Additionally, wild indigo has been shown to regulate appetite and metabolism, helping individuals feel more satisfied after meals and reducing the likelihood of overindulging. By promoting a healthier digestive balance, wild indigo tinctures can help break the cycle of overeating and support long-term weight management.
remedy_properties: Digestive Soothing Properties: Wild Indigo's anti-inflammatory properties can calm digestive issues, reducing discomfort and bloating associated with overeating, allowing for a more balanced diet.

Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Effects: Wild Indigo's antioxidant compounds may help reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to cravings and overeating by promoting overall health and well-being.
Blood Sugar Regulation: Wild Indigo has been traditionally used to regulate blood sugar levels, which can help curb overeating caused by spikes or dips in blood sugar levels.
remedy_constituents: Isoquinoline alkaloids: These compounds may help regulate appetite and digestion by interacting with the nervous system to reduce cravings for food.

Flavonoids: As antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents, flavonoids in Baptisia tinctoria may help stabilize blood sugar levels and reduce inflammation associated with overeating, promoting a healthier appetite and digestion.
Coumarins: Coumarins in Baptisia tinctoria may aid in appetite regulation by influencing the gut microbiome and enhancing the body's natural ability to manage hunger and satiety.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for their bitter properties to stimulate digestive function and reduce appetite.
Leaves: Used for their astringent and bitter properties to slow down digestion and reduce food cravings.
Seeds: Used for their bitter properties to stimulate digestive function and reduce appetite.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of baptisia tinctoria roots and leaves in late summer or early fall when plant is in full bloom.
Clean the baptisia tinctoria plant material by gently brushing off dirt and debris with a soft brush.
Steep the baptisia tinctoria plant material in 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar for 4-6 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding the solids.
Store the finished baptisia tinctoria tincture in a cool dark place for at least 2 weeks before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with overeating because it targets the emotional roots of food cravings. The herb glycyrrhizin, a key compound in licorice root, has been shown to calm the nervous system and reduce stress-induced eating behaviors. Additionally, licorice tincture's sweet flavor can help curb sugar cravings, making it an effective natural remedy for those who struggle with emotional eating. By addressing these underlying issues, herbal licorice tinctures promote a healthier relationship with food and support long-term weight management.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has been shown to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for overeating. By calming the nervous system, glycyrrhizin can help regulate appetite and food cravings.

Triterpenoids: These compounds have been found to slow gastric emptying, reducing feelings of hunger and satiety. This can help individuals feel fuller longer and reduce the urge to snack or overeat between meals.

Flavonoids: Licorice tincture contains flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe digestive issues like bloating, cramps, and discomfort. By reducing inflammation in the gut, flavonoids can alleviate symptoms associated with overeating and promote a healthy appetite.
remedy_constituents: Licorice Glycosides: These flavonoid glycosides help with overeating by reducing inflammation in the digestive system, which can contribute to overeating behaviors and digestive discomfort.
Glycyrrhetic Acid: This triterpenoid acid may help with overeating by reducing cortisol levels, which can contribute to increased appetite and food cravings.
Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin may help with overeating by reducing stress and anxiety, which can contribute to overeating behaviors as a coping mechanism.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make tinctures for overeating due to their high glycyrrhizin content, which helps to balance fluid retention and reduce water retention caused by overeating.
Roots: The roots are also used to aid in digestion and reduce symptoms of overeating such as bloating and indigestion.
Roots: Additionally, the roots are used to help alleviate stress and anxiety that may contribute to overeating behaviors, promoting a healthier relationship with food.
remedy_recipe: Collect and clean 2-3 pounds of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots with no signs of mold or mildew.
Chop the roots into small pieces using a sharp knife or a root cutter for easier extraction.
Combine the chopped roots with 80% ethanol in a 1:5 ratio by weight in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture for 2-4 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking of the container.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with overeating because it supports digestive health by soothing inflammation in the gut, reducing discomfort and bloating associated with eating large meals. The mucilages found in marshmallow root create a protective barrier that cushions the stomach lining, allowing for easier digestion and absorption of nutrients. Additionally, the tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help regulate appetite hormones, making it easier to stop overindulging and develop healthier eating habits.
remedy_properties: Mucilage: Marshmallow's mucilage property helps to soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to overeating and discomfort.

Demulcent: The demulcent action of marshmallow tincture creates a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, allowing for easy passage of nutrients while also preventing irritation and inflammation, which can help regulate appetite and reduce overeating.

Antioxidant: Marshmallow's antioxidant properties may help to reduce oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to cravings for unhealthy foods and excessive eating by reducing inflammation and promoting overall health.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages help to soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing inflammation and irritation that can lead to overeating and digestive discomfort.
Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids, particularly ursolic acid, have been shown to have appetite-suppressing and weight-loss-promoting effects, helping to regulate eating habits and reduce overeating.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly quercetin, have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation in the digestive system, which can contribute to overeating and weight gain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary part used for making tinctures as they contain a high concentration of mucilages, which help to soothe and calm the digestive system.
Leaves: The leaves are also used for making tinctures as they contain similar properties to the roots, aiding in digestion and reducing inflammation in the digestive tract.

Stems: The stems of Althaea officinalis are sometimes used for making tinctures, although less commonly than the roots and leaves, as they still contain some mucilages and other beneficial compounds that can aid in digestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried roots of althaea officinalis in the early morning to ensure maximum potency.
Chop the harvested roots into smaller pieces to increase their surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped roots with 5 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks to allow for proper extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a separate container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with overeating because it stimulates digestive enzymes and improves digestion, reducing bloating and discomfort after meals. The menthol in peppermint also has a calming effect on the stomach, which can help alleviate feelings of nausea and indigestion often associated with overindulgence. Additionally, peppermint's ability to slow down gastric emptying allows for more thorough digestion, providing a sense of fullness and satisfaction, making it easier to stop eating when needed. This natural remedy promotes healthy appetite regulation and supports a balanced diet.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Peppermint tincture stimulates digestion, reducing bloating and discomfort after eating, which can help individuals avoid overindulging due to discomfort.

Carminative Agent: Peppermint's natural oils have carminative properties, which relieve gas and bloating, reducing the urge to snack or eat more due to uncomfortable symptoms like flatulence or indigestion.

Appetite Suppressant: The menthol content in peppermint tincture can help suppress appetite by stimulating the digestive system and improving nutrient absorption, leading to a sense of fullness and satisfaction.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol helps with overeating by suppressing appetite and reducing cravings for food, which can lead to weight loss.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid helps with overeating by inhibiting the growth of certain bacteria in the gut that can contribute to overeating and weight gain, improving digestion and reducing inflammation.
Piperitone: Piperitone helps with overeating by having a satiating effect, reducing food intake, and potentially aiding in weight loss by reducing body fat.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used to make tinctures due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which can help alleviate digestive discomfort and reduce hunger.
Roots: The roots are used to make tinctures because they contain a higher concentration of menthone and other compounds that may help regulate appetite and improve digestion.
Stems: The stems are used to make tinctures as they contain essential oils and compounds that may help reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive issues associated with overeating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers at peak potency for best results.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped plant material with 50-100 ml of vodka or 70-proof ethanol in a clean glass container.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking occasionally.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon tinctures helps with overeating because of its unique ability to regulate blood sugar levels and suppress appetite. The cinnamaldehyde compound in Ceylon cinnamon stimulates the release of insulin, which helps to reduce cravings for sugary foods and drinks that can contribute to overeating. Additionally, the tincture's natural sweetness satisfies cravings without adding refined sugars to the diet. As a result, individuals who consume herbal Ceylon cinnamon tinctures may find it easier to manage their portion sizes and make healthier food choices.
remedy_properties: Cinnamaldehyde: This compound has been shown to reduce sugar cravings by increasing the release of peptide YY, a hormone that helps regulate appetite and satiety.
Methoxy cinnamic aldehyde: This compound has been found to slow down gastric emptying, which can help individuals feel fuller for longer and reduce the likelihood of overeating.
Coumarin: This antioxidant has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce cravings for unhealthy foods and increase feelings of fullness by reducing inflammation in the body.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: Acts as a natural appetite suppressant, reducing the desire to overeat by slowing down digestion and reducing inflammation.
Coumarin: Exhibits a satiety-inducing effect, helping to reduce hunger and food cravings, and may also aid in weight loss by improving metabolism.
Cinnamic acid: May help in regulating blood sugar levels, reducing cravings for high-carbohydrate foods, and promoting feelings of fullness, which can help combat overeating.
remedy_parts: Buds: Used to make tinctures for overeating due to its potential to aid in digestion and reduce appetite.
Leaves: Used to make tinctures for overeating as they may help stimulate digestion and reduce cravings for food.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 cups of dried cinnamomum verum bark, and 1 cup of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Combine the cinnamomum verum bark with 2 cups of vodka in a clean glass jar, making sure the bark is completely submerged.
Seal the jar and let it steep in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Store the cinnamomum verum tincture in a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid, in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with overeating because it has a natural appetite-suppressing effect, which can help to curb cravings for unhealthy snacks. The spicy compounds in ginger stimulate the digestive system, reducing feelings of hunger and bloating. Additionally, ginger's warming properties can increase circulation and metabolism, allowing the body to process food more efficiently. This combination helps to reduce overindulgence and promote healthier eating habits, making it an effective natural remedy for those struggling with portion control.
remedy_properties: Digestive enzymes stimulant: Ginger stimulates digestive enzymes, which helps break down food more efficiently and reduces feelings of bloating and discomfort after eating. This can help reduce overeating by making it easier to digest and process food.

Anti-inflammatory compound: Ginger contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the body, which is often associated with conditions like irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and leaky gut, both of which can contribute to overeating.

Carminative agent: Ginger has carminative properties, meaning it helps relieve bloating and gas by reducing the formation of gas in the digestive system. This can help reduce discomfort and reduce the desire to eat more than necessary.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These iridoid glycosides are responsible for ginger's anti-inflammatory and digestive properties, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with overeating, such as nausea and indigestion.

Shogaols: These compounds, similar to gingerols, have anti-inflammatory and digestive benefits, and may help to reduce feelings of hunger and fullness by regulating digestive enzymes and improving gut health.

6-Gingerol: This specific gingerol has been shown to have a satiety-inducing effect, potentially reducing the likelihood of overeating by slowing down gastric emptying and reducing hunger signals in the brain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are also used due to their similar composition to rhizomes, providing a more readily available alternative for tincture production.
Stems is not in the list of common parts. Leaves: Leaves are not in the list of common parts either.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh zingiber officinale roots with minimal damage to preserve their medicinal properties immediately.
Chop the harvested roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for better solvent penetration.
Combine 2 cups of the chopped roots with 2 cups of 80 proof ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks to allow the solvent to extract the roots' medicinal properties.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to separate the tincture from the solids and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kava tinctures helps with overeating because it has a unique ability to reduce anxiety and stress, which are often underlying triggers for emotional eating. When we feel anxious or stressed, our brain sends signals to our stomach to release serotonin, a neurotransmitter that stimulates hunger and cravings. Kava tinctures can help calm the nervous system, reducing these signals and promoting a sense of relaxation and fullness. As a result, individuals using kava tinctures may find themselves eating less frequently and feeling more satisfied between meals.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) effects: Kava's anxiolytic properties can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for overeating.
Sedative and calming effects: The sedative properties of kava can promote relaxation and reduce feelings of restlessness or agitation that may contribute to overeating.
Muscle relaxant properties: Kava's muscle-relaxing effects can help alleviate digestive discomfort and bloating, which can be alleviated with kava consumption.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the kava plant are a popular choice for making tinctures as they contain the highest concentration of active compounds, such as kavalactones, which help regulate appetite and reduce cravings.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used because they have a higher concentration of flavonoids, which have been shown to aid in weight management by reducing fat absorption and increasing metabolism.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh kava rhizomes roots or leaves dried kava.
Cut the harvested material into small pieces and combine with 500 milliliters of high-proof ethanol.
Steep the mixture in a clean glass jar for 14 days away from direct sunlight and moisture.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding solids.
Store the resulting piper methysticum tincture in a cool dark place for up to two years after opening.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle tinctures helps with overeating because it calms the nervous system, reducing cravings and emotional eating. The anti-inflammatory properties of nettle also aid in digestion, alleviating symptoms of bloating and discomfort that can trigger overindulgence. Furthermore, the vitamins and minerals present in nettle, such as vitamin K and calcium, support a healthy appetite response, allowing individuals to listen to their true hunger cues rather than relying on emotional or stress-driven eating habits.
remedy_properties: Digestive Support: Nettle's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties can soothe digestive issues, reducing discomfort and bloating that often leads to overeating.

Appetite Suppression: The natural compounds in nettle, such as flavonoids and terpenes, have been shown to help regulate appetite and reduce cravings for unhealthy foods, making it easier to manage portion sizes.
Anxiety Relief: Nettle's calming effects can help alleviate stress and anxiety-related eating, reducing the likelihood of overindulging in comfort foods or emotional snacking.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the stinging nettle plant contain a compound called lupeol, which helps to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation in the stomach, making it effective for treating overeating.
Leaves: The leaves of the stinging nettle plant have been traditionally used to treat indigestion and bloating caused by overeating due to their ability to relax the muscles of the stomach.
remedy_recipe: Harvest urtica dioica leaves and stems in the morning after dew has evaporated using scissors or pruning shears.
Dry the plant material in a warm dark place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content by half using paper bags or cloth bundles.
Chop the dried plant material into small pieces and pack them tightly into glass jars leaving about an inch of headspace.
Pour high-proof vodka over the plant material filling the jar to the top and seal with tight-fitting lids allowing steeping for 6-8 weeks.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into dark glass bottles discarding the solids and labeling the bottles with ingredients dosages and preparation dates.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary tinctures helps with overeating because it stimulates digestion and improves appetite regulation. The herbal extract increases the release of digestive enzymes, allowing the body to break down food more efficiently and reduce symptoms of indigestion and bloating. Additionally, rosemary has been shown to suppress hunger hormones, reducing cravings for unhealthy snacks and promoting a sense of fullness and satisfaction. By taking control of digestion and appetite, rosemary tinctures can help individuals develop healthier eating habits and overcome overeating patterns.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Rosemary's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which may contribute to overeating behaviors.

Stimulates digestion and appetite regulation: Rosemary's essential oils, particularly camphor and bornyl acetate, have been shown to improve digestion and regulate appetite, helping to prevent overeating and support healthy weight management.

Cognitive enhancement and focus: Rosemary's cognitive-boosting compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, can help improve mental clarity, focus, and memory, which may reduce the likelihood of mindless or emotional eating.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots are rich in antioxidants and have a calming effect on the digestive system, making them effective in reducing symptoms of overeating.
Seeds: Rosemary seeds contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help alleviate digestive discomfort and reduce inflammation caused by excessive food consumption.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the morning after sunrise and before midday.
Chop 100g of harvested leaves into small pieces using scissors or a mortar and pestle.
Combine chopped leaves with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar and steep for 14 days.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container after 14 days.
Store the tincture in a dark glass bottle with tight-fitting lid and label it clearly after filtering.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen tinctures helps with overeating because it has a natural ability to slow down digestion, reducing the likelihood of consuming large portions. The compound methyl salicylate in wintergreen tincture also helps to curb cravings for sweet and salty foods, making it easier to make healthier choices. Additionally, wintergreen's calming properties can soothe emotional eating triggers, promoting a sense of fullness and satisfaction after meals. By addressing both physical and emotional hunger cues, herbal wintergreen tinctures can be a valuable tool in managing overeating habits.
remedy_properties: Menthol: Wintergreen tincture's menthol content helps to reduce hunger pangs by numbing the stomach lining, making food feel less appealing and reducing cravings.

Methyl Salicylate: The presence of methyl salicylate in wintergreen tinctures helps to stimulate digestion, improve nutrient absorption, and reduce bloating, which can alleviate feelings of fullness and discomfort after eating.
Glycosides: Wintergreen's glycoside content has been shown to help regulate appetite hormones, such as ghrelin and leptin, which can lead to decreased hunger and improved weight management.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of wintergreen contain high concentrations of methyl salicylate, a natural pain reliever that helps alleviate discomfort associated with overeating.
Rhyzomes: Wintergreen rhyzomes have been used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues, including those caused by overindulgence, due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems in the morning or evening when dew is present.
Chop the plant material into small pieces and combine it with 40% vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture at room temperature for 4-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to macerate the herbs.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.
Label and date the tincture, which is now ready to use as a dietary supplement in recommended dosages of 20-30 drops three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cassia tinctures helps with overeating because it contains a compound called cinnamic acid, which has been shown to slow down gastric emptying, reducing hunger and cravings for unhealthy foods. Additionally, cassia's natural diuretic properties help to balance blood sugar levels, making it easier to resist the temptation of overindulgence. By addressing both physical and emotional triggers of overeating, cassia tinctures can support a healthier relationship with food and promote sustainable weight management.
remedy_properties: Stomachic and Digestive Properties: Cassia tincture helps to stimulate digestive enzymes, reducing bloating and discomfort associated with overeating. This allows for more efficient nutrient absorption and alleviates symptoms like indigestion.

Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Effects: Cassia's antioxidant properties help to reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to cravings and overeating. By soothing inflammation, Cassia tincture may help regulate appetite and improve overall health.

Stress-Reducing and Sedative Properties: The sedative compounds found in Cassia tincture can help calm the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that often trigger overeating habits. By promoting relaxation, Cassia can support healthy eating habits and weight management.
remedy_parts: Roots: Cassia roots have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues and reduce appetite, making them a popular choice for tinctures aimed at reducing overeating.
Barks: The bark of the cassia plant contains compounds that help to regulate blood sugar levels and suppress hunger, making it an effective ingredient in tinctures for managing overeating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest senna auriculata leaves and flowers fresh from the plant ensuring proper identification.
Dry the harvested material in a warm dry place for at least seven days or until brittle.
Chop the dried material into small pieces and combine with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar filling to 90% capacity.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six weeks shaking the jar twice daily allowing maceration and infusion.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter discarding the solids and transferring the tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Indian snakeroot tinctures helps with overeating because they contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds that target the root causes of emotional eating. remedy_properties: Anthelmintic properties: Indian Snakeroot's anthelmintic properties can help reduce parasite infestations in the digestive system, which can contribute to cravings and overeating by disrupting gut health and hormone regulation.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds present in Indian Snakeroot tinctures can help soothe digestive issues such as bloating, cramps, and discomfort that may trigger overeating behaviors.
Sedative and calming effects: Indian Snakeroot's sedative and calming properties can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common underlying causes of emotional eating and overeating.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rauvolfia serpentina roots and clean them thoroughly with water.
Dry the cleaned roots in a warm oven at 150°f for 4-6 hours or until crispy.
Grind dried root material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix 1 part ground rauvolfia serpentina powder with 5 parts vodka or neutral grain spirit in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily, before straining and filtering the tincture.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achyranthes aspera roots at dawn in late summer or early fall for optimal potency.
Cleanly chop 20 grams of dried or fresh roots into small pieces for efficient extraction.
Combine chopped root material with 100 milliliters of high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks allowing for gentle maceration and infusion.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding solids and retaining tincture.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for overeating are concentrated plant extracts used to help manage excessive eating. They work by regulating appetite, improving digestion, and enhancing metabolism. The best herbal tinctures for overeating include Gymnema sylvestre, Ginkgo biloba, Cassia auriculata, Garcinia cambogia, Silybum marianum, Gardenia jasminoides, Ilex paraguariensis, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Ulmus rubra, and Catha edulis. If left untreated, overeating can lead to weight-related health issues, decreased self-esteem, and strained relationships, ultimately affecting overall quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to overeating that are treated with herbal tinctures are digestive disorders such as bloating, indigestion, and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Herbs like ginger, peppermint, and dandelion root can help alleviate symptoms of discomfort, gas, and bloating. Other herbs like turmeric, chamomile, and licorice root may soothe inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, promoting healthy digestion and alleviating digestive discomfort similar to that experienced with overeating.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with overeating is a blend of Peppermint, Fennel, and Ginger. Peppermint helps to calm the digestive system and reduce cravings, while Fennel supports healthy digestion and reduces bloating. Ginger, with its warming properties, aids in reducing nausea and stabilizing blood sugar levels. This synergy promotes a balanced appetite, reduces emotional eating, and encourages mindful eating habits, ultimately leading to a healthier relationship with food.

As a result, individuals may experience a decrease in their desire to overindulge in sugary or high-calorie foods. The tincture also helps to regulate blood sugar levels, which can lead to increased feelings of fullness and satisfaction, making it easier to maintain a healthy eating habit and avoid overeating. This ultimately supports weight management and overall well-being.

The increased circulation may also support the brain's ability to recognize and respond to hunger and fullness cues, leading to more mindful eating habits. Additionally, ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, potentially contributing to a healthier relationship with food and reduced overeating. Regular use may promote a more balanced eating pattern.

Additionally, Garcinia cambogia has been shown to decrease levels of ghrelin, a hormone that stimulates appetite, leading to a reduction in overeating and a more balanced eating habit.
